\bYou want some general tips? Hmm. Best tip I can give here is to expect the unexpected. They don't play by the meta of the main series game, they play by PoMas's meta.

\bFor example, they'll buff their stats up by a lot more than they probably should, invent new mechanics, ignore Protect and heal more than they should, just to name a few things.
\bFor example, they'll buff their stats up by a lot more than they probably should, invent new mechanics, ignore Protect and heal more than they should, just to name a few things.
\bAlso, about some of their "moves". They're not used by the Pokémon, they're used by the trainer, so they can be used even when the Pokémon's been afflicted with a condition that would normally stop them from moving.
\bAlso, about some of their "moves". They're not used by the Pokémon, they're used by the trainer, so they can be used even when the Pokémon's been afflicted with a condition that would normally stop them from moving.
\bLastly, cheese moves like Destiny Bond and Perish Song won't work here; Knock Off also won't knock off their items. Don't try them!
\bLastly, cheese moves like Destiny Bond and Perish Song won't work here; Knock Off also won't knock off their items. Don't try them!

\bIn any case, good luck, you'll need it.
\bSure, I'll explain them.\nCrit Rate - how often a Pokémon lands a crit, up to a maximum of 3 stages.\nTrainer Move - A move that the trainer uses, which provide buffs to their Pokémon.
\bSure, I'll explain them.\nCrit Rate - how often a Pokémon lands a crit, up to a maximum of 3 stages.\nTrainer Move - A move that the trainer uses, which provide buffs to their Pokémon.
\bB Move - Stands for Buddy Move. These are similar to regular moves, except they have a B in front of the move's name. They usually do what the regular move does, with increased power or added effects or the regular effect enhanced.\nPassive Skill - Essentially an ability. How many a Pokémon has here is completely arbitrary.
\bB Move - Stands for Buddy Move. These are similar to regular moves, except they have a B in front of the move's name. They usually do what the regular move does, with increased power or added effects or the regular effect enhanced.\nPassive Skill - Essentially an ability. How many a Pokémon has here is completely arbitrary.
\bMaster Passive - A Passive Skill that increases the Pokémon's damage and reduces the damage it takes.\nZone - A field effect like a weather or terrain, boosting moves of a certain type.
\bMaster Passive - A Passive Skill that increases the Pokémon's damage and reduces the damage it takes.\nZone - A field effect like a weather or terrain, boosting moves of a certain type.
\bGradual Healing - The Pokémon recovers 1/16th of their max HP every turn.
\bGradual Healing - The Pokémon recovers 1/16th of their max HP every turn.
